title: hoya fetuana subsp. fetuana
intro: Hoya fetuana subsp. fetuana, also known as the Red Waxy Hoya, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical forests of the Solomon Islands. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its extracts and compounds exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hoya fetuana subsp. fetuana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
